questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other traffic is present
Yield to traffic on your left
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to make a complete stop and wait until the signal turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn green
You may turn left only
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red, and you should stop if it is safe rather than trying to beat the light.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic signal mean?
choices: Stop completely, then proceed when safe
Proceed without stopping
Slow down but do not stop
The signal is out of order and should be ignored
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: come to a full stop and go only when it is safe.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagonal) red sign always means:
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
Speed limit
questions.explanation: The octagonal shape and red color are reserved exclusively for stop signs.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: Passing is not allowed
Passing is allowed when clear
The road ends ahead
You must turn left
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side indicates that passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
choices: Yield the right-of-way
Stop completely
No passing
Construction ahead
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: Diamond-shaped signs are generally used to:
choices: Warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead
Give regulatory speed limits
Mark interstate routes
Indicate services and food
questions.explanation: Diamond-shaped warning signs alert drivers to hazards such as curves, intersections, or other conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: Orange-colored traffic signs usually indicate:
choices: Construction or work zones
School zones
Hospital locations
Rest areas
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markers identify construction and maintenance work zones where extra caution is needed.

questions.prompt: When a police officer is directing traffic at an intersection with a working signal, you should:
choices: Obey the officer even if it conflicts with the signal
Follow the signal and ignore the officer
Stop and wait for the signal to change
Choose whichever instruction is faster
questions.explanation: Directions from a police officer always take priority over traffic signals and signs.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Traffic moving in the same direction
Traffic moving in opposite directions
Parking spaces from travel lanes
Bicycle lanes from sidewalks
questions.explanation: White lines divide lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The larger vehicle
Whoever signals first
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and crossing pedestrians
Only vehicles behind you
No one, because you are turning
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and sirens on, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a private driveway or alley must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road
Proceed because driveways have the right-of-way
Honk and continue
Stop only if a sign requires it
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from driveways, alleys, or private roads must yield to traffic already traveling on the roadway.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to vehicles already in the intersection and to the driver on your right
Always go first if you arrive on a paved road
Speed up to claim the right-of-way
Stop and wait for all other traffic
questions.explanation: Without signs or signals, yield to traffic already in the intersection and to the vehicle on your right when arriving together.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is crossing in a marked crosswalk, drivers must:
choices: Yield the right-of-way to the pedestrian
Honk to warn the pedestrian
Proceed if the pedestrian is not in your lane yet
Drive around the pedestrian
questions.explanation: Drivers are required to yield to pedestrians in crosswalks.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, you should signal:
choices: Well in advance so others can react
Only after you begin the turn
Just as your bumper reaches the corner
Only if other cars are nearby
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers and pedestrians time to anticipate your move.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you should:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the circle
Stop in the circle to let others enter
Enter quickly before checking for traffic
Always turn left into the circle
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right-of-way, so entering drivers must yield.

questions.prompt: Even when you legally have the right-of-way, you should:
choices: Be prepared to yield to avoid a crash
Insist on going first no matter what
Never slow down
Assume all drivers will stop for you
questions.explanation: Right-of-way must be given, not taken, and a safe driver yields when needed to prevent a collision.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should generally turn from and into:
choices: The lane nearest the right curb
The center lane
Any open lane
The left lane
questions.explanation: Right turns should begin and end in the lane closest to the right-hand curb or edge of the road.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, unless otherwise posted, the speed limit on interstate and controlled-access highways is generally:
choices: 70 mph
55 mph
65 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: Tennessee interstates and controlled-access highways generally allow speeds up to 70 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: The basic speed law requires you to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is safe for current conditions
Always drive exactly the posted limit
Drive as fast as traffic allows
Match the speed of the fastest car
questions.explanation: The basic speed rule means you must adjust your speed to road, weather, and traffic conditions, even below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: A good rule for judging a safe following distance in good conditions is the:
choices: Three-second rule
One-car-length rule
Half-second rule
Ten-second rule
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep enough space to react and stop behind the vehicle ahead.

questions.prompt: In a typical Tennessee urban or business district, the default speed limit is usually about:
choices: 30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
20 mph
questions.explanation: Unless posted otherwise, the default speed limit in urban and business districts is about 30 mph.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: Roads are wet, icy, or visibility is poor
Traffic is light
You are in a hurry
The road is dry and straight
questions.explanation: Slippery or low-visibility conditions increase stopping distance, so you need more space between vehicles.

questions.prompt: If a driver is following you too closely (tailgating), you should:
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead
Brake hard to warn them
Speed up well above the limit
Stop in the road
questions.explanation: Leaving more room ahead gives you space to brake gradually so the tailgater has time to react.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, your vehicle's stopping distance:
choices: Increases
Decreases
Stays the same
Becomes zero
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require greater distance to stop because both reaction and braking distances grow.

questions.prompt: When approaching a sharp curve, you should:
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Speed up through the curve
Coast in neutral
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps you keep control, since braking hard while turning can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Can block traffic flow and cause crashes
Always saves fuel
Is never a violation
Improves following distance for others
questions.explanation: Going too slowly interrupts the normal flow of traffic and can lead to dangerous passing and rear-end collisions.

questions.prompt: When merging onto a highway, you should:
choices: Match the speed of highway traffic and merge into a gap
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Merge slowly regardless of traffic speed
Force other vehicles to slow for you
questions.explanation: Accelerating to the speed of traffic lets you blend smoothly into an available gap.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane should primarily be used for:
choices: Passing slower vehicles
Driving slowly
Parking
Stopping to rest
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right, leaving the left lane for passing.

questions.prompt: You may not pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
You are on a straight road
The road has two lanes in your direction
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side prohibits passing because it may not be safe to do so.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Check mirrors, signal, and check your blind spot
Only use your mirrors
Signal after you move over
Close your eyes and steer
questions.explanation: Checking mirrors and your blind spot while signaling helps you change lanes safely.

questions.prompt: It is safe to return to your lane after passing when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rear-view mirror
Your front bumper is even with the other car
You begin the pass
You are alongside the other vehicle
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to return to your lane safely.

questions.prompt: While passing another vehicle, you should:
choices: Not exceed the posted speed limit
Drive as fast as needed
Turn off your signal
Drive in the other car's blind spot
questions.explanation: Passing does not give you permission to exceed the speed limit; you must complete the pass within the legal speed.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally prohibited:
choices: At railroad crossings and intersections
On any straight road
On multi-lane highways
Anytime traffic is light
questions.explanation: Passing at intersections, railroad crossings, and other restricted areas is dangerous and not allowed.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move toward the center line
Brake suddenly
questions.explanation: Keeping a steady speed or slowing a bit lets the passing vehicle complete its maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: A broken white line between lanes means:
choices: You may change lanes when it is safe
You may never cross it
The road ends ahead
Traffic flows in opposite directions
questions.explanation: A broken white line separates same-direction lanes and allows lane changes when safe.

questions.prompt: Weaving in and out of traffic to pass multiple cars is:
choices: Dangerous and may be cited as reckless driving
A normal way to save time
Allowed on interstates
Required when traffic is heavy
questions.explanation: Frequent unsafe lane changes endanger others and can result in a reckless driving charge.

questions.prompt: When you want to pass a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them plenty of room and pass only when safe
Pass closely and quickly
Honk and pass immediately
Pass on a curve to get it over with
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are vulnerable, so allow ample space and pass only when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: When you see a school bus stopped with red lights flashing and its stop arm extended, you must:
choices: Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing
Pass slowly on the left
Honk and continue
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights and an extended stop arm mean children are loading or unloading and you must stop.

questions.prompt: Tennessee's Move Over Law requires you to do what when approaching a stopped emergency or utility vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Move to a non-adjacent lane or slow down if you cannot
Speed up to pass quickly
Maintain speed in the same lane
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law requires changing lanes away from the stopped vehicle, or slowing down when you cannot move over safely.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering beside or directly behind them
Drive closely behind to save fuel
Stay in the truck's blind spot
Pass on the right whenever possible
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you, so avoid staying in those blind spots.

questions.prompt: Because motorcycles are smaller and harder to see, drivers should:
choices: Look twice at intersections and allow them a full lane
Share the lane beside them
Follow them closely
Assume they will always yield
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are easy to miss, so checking carefully and giving them a full lane helps prevent crashes.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the road:
choices: Have the same rights and duties as drivers
Must always ride on the sidewalk
Have no legal right to the road
Can be ignored by motorists
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are considered roadway users with the same general rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers.

questions.prompt: When making a turn, you should check for pedestrians and cyclists because:
choices: They may be crossing or riding where you are turning
They always have to stop for cars
They are easy to see in all conditions
They never use crosswalks
questions.explanation: Pedestrians and cyclists can be in your path during a turn, so you must look for them before proceeding.

questions.prompt: When a large truck is making a wide right turn ahead of you, you should:
choices: Stay back and not try to squeeze between the truck and the curb
Pass on the right side
Move up close to the truck
Honk and go around on the left only
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn, and squeezing in on the right can put you in a dangerous blind spot.

questions.prompt: At night, when an oncoming vehicle approaches, you should:
choices: Dim your high beams to low beams
Keep your high beams on
Flash your high beams repeatedly
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: Switching to low beams prevents blinding oncoming drivers and helps everyone see the road.

questions.prompt: When you approach a pedestrian who is blind and using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Yield and stop to let them cross safely
Honk to alert them
Drive around quickly
Continue without slowing
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield the right-of-way to pedestrians who are blind, giving them time to cross safely.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, the adult legal limit for blood alcohol concentration (BAC) while driving is:
questions.explanation: It is illegal for an adult driver in Tennessee to operate a vehicle with a BAC of 0.08% or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Tennessee, the zero-tolerance BAC limit is:
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 can be charged under Tennessee's zero-tolerance law at a BAC of just 0.02%.

questions.prompt: Under Tennessee's Hands Free Law, drivers are prohibited from:
choices: Holding or physically supporting a phone while driving
Using a hands-free device
Talking to passengers
Listening to the radio
questions.explanation: The Hands Free Law makes it illegal to hold a phone with your hand or body while driving.

questions.prompt: The only thing that will reduce the alcohol level in your body over time is:
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Exercise
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to remove alcohol; coffee and other remedies do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: Texting or reading messages while driving is dangerous because it:
choices: Takes your eyes, hands, and mind off driving
Improves reaction time
Helps you stay alert
Has no effect on driving
questions.explanation: Texting combines visual, manual, and mental distraction, greatly increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Improving coordination
Sharpening vision
Making you a safer driver
questions.explanation: Alcohol impairs reaction time, judgment, and coordination, all of which are needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: Prescription or over-the-counter medications can:
choices: Impair your ability to drive safely
Never affect driving
Only affect commercial drivers
Improve your driving
questions.explanation: Many medications cause drowsiness or other effects that can make driving unsafe, so read warning labels.

questions.prompt: For commercial drivers in Tennessee, the legal BAC limit while driving is:
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter 0.04% BAC limit when operating a commercial vehicle.

questions.prompt: The safest choice when you plan to drink alcohol is to:
choices: Arrange a sober driver or other transportation
Drive carefully after drinking
Drink only a little before driving
Wait ten minutes before driving
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride is the only reliable way to avoid driving while impaired.

questions.prompt: Besides phone use, another common form of distracted driving is:
choices: Eating or adjusting controls while driving
Keeping both hands on the wheel
Watching the road ahead
Checking your mirrors
questions.explanation: Any activity that takes your attention from driving, such as eating or fiddling with controls, is a distraction.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, you must turn on your headlights:
choices: Whenever weather requires the use of windshield wipers
Only at night
Only on interstates
Only when it is snowing
questions.explanation: Tennessee law requires headlights to be on any time conditions make windshield wipers necessary.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams are the correct choice.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and keep the wheel steady
Brake hard immediately
Accelerate to regain control
Turn the wheel sharply
questions.explanation: Gradually reducing speed and holding the wheel steady lets the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: Bridges and overpasses are especially dangerous in cold weather because they:
choices: Freeze before other road surfaces
Are always wider
Have better traction
Never collect ice
questions.explanation: Cold air circulates around bridges, so they ice over before regular roadways.

questions.prompt: The road can be most slippery:
choices: During the first rain after a dry period
After it has rained for hours
Only when it snows
When the road is dry
questions.explanation: Oil and dirt rise to the surface when rain first falls, making roads especially slick.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain or reduced visibility, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
Maintain highway speed
Use your high beams
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility require slower speeds and extra following distance.

questions.prompt: At night, you should drive so that you can stop within:
choices: The distance lighted by your headlights
Twice your headlight range
Any distance you choose
The length of the highway
questions.explanation: Overdriving your headlights means you cannot stop in time for hazards you see too late, so keep your speed in check.

questions.prompt: If a skid causes the rear of your car to slide, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the front to go
Brake hard and hold the wheel still
Accelerate quickly
Turn the wheel the opposite way
questions.explanation: Steering gently in the direction you want to go helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: To see and be seen in poor weather, you should keep your:
choices: Windshield, mirrors, and lights clean
Windows fogged for comfort
High beams on at all times
Wipers off to save them
questions.explanation: Clean glass and lights improve both your visibility and how well others can see you.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels away from the curb uphill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls back.

questions.prompt: When parking downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
Slightly left
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb downhill lets the curb block the car if it rolls forward.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: On a crosswalk or in front of a fire hydrant
In a marked parking lot
On a residential street where allowed
In a driveway you own
questions.explanation: Parking on crosswalks, sidewalks, or near fire hydrants blocks safety access and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parallel parking space, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and cyclists
Pull out immediately
Honk and go
Reverse without looking
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking for approaching traffic and cyclists helps you re-enter the roadway safely.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space marked for people with disabilities only if you:
choices: Have a valid disability permit or plate
Are running a quick errand
Cannot find another space
Leave your flashers on
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces are reserved for vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: After parking, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park or into gear
Leave the car in neutral
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors open
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and shifting properly keeps your vehicle from rolling while parked.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street, you should:
choices: Check for traffic and bicyclists
Open it quickly
Assume the lane is clear
Open it only on the traffic side
questions.explanation: Opening a door into traffic can cause a crash, so always look for approaching vehicles and cyclists first.

questions.prompt: You should not stop or park where:
choices: You would block the normal flow of traffic
A legal space is provided
Signs allow parking
There is a wide shoulder for emergencies
questions.explanation: Stopping where you obstruct traffic is unsafe and prohibited except in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When you parallel park, you should:
choices: Back in slowly while watching your mirrors and surroundings
Pull in nose first quickly
Avoid using your mirrors
Park without signaling
questions.explanation: Backing in slowly with full awareness of your surroundings lets you fit the space without hitting other cars.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle breaks down on a highway, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Stop in the travel lane
Leave the car in the road and walk away
Keep driving on a flat tire
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the roadway and using hazard lights warns others and keeps you safer.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, you may apply for a learner permit at what minimum age?
questions.explanation: Tennessee allows you to apply for a learner permit at age 15 after passing the required tests.

questions.prompt: A Tennessee learner permit holder may drive only when accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front seat
Any licensed friend
A passenger over 18 in any seat
No one, if driving in daytime
questions.explanation: Permit holders must have a licensed driver who is at least 21 seated beside them in the front seat.

questions.prompt: With a Tennessee learner permit, you may not drive between the hours of:
choices: 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
11 p.m. and 5 a.m.
Midnight and 5 a.m.
9 p.m. and 6 a.m.
questions.explanation: Learner permit holders are restricted from driving between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.

questions.prompt: How long must you hold a Tennessee learner permit before advancing to the Intermediate Restricted License?
choices: At least 180 days
30 days
90 days
One year
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires you to hold the learner permit for a minimum of 180 days before moving up.

questions.prompt: To advance to the Intermediate Restricted License, a Tennessee teen must certify how many hours of supervised driving?
choices: 50 hours, including 10 at night
20 hours total
100 hours, all in daytime
10 hours total
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires 50 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of those at night.

questions.prompt: A Tennessee Intermediate Restricted License generally allows the driver to carry:
choices: Only one passenger (with some family exceptions)
Up to four passengers
Any number of passengers
No passengers ever
questions.explanation: The intermediate license limits the driver to one passenger, apart from certain family exceptions.

questions.prompt: The Tennessee written knowledge test for first-time drivers has how many questions?
questions.explanation: The Tennessee knowledge test consists of 30 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: To pass the Tennessee written knowledge test, you must answer at least:
choices: 80% of the questions correctly
60% of the questions correctly
70% of the questions correctly
90% of the questions correctly
questions.explanation: A passing score on the Tennessee knowledge test is 80%, or 24 of 30 questions correct.

questions.prompt: Whenever you drive, you must:
choices: Carry your valid permit or license
Leave your license at home
Carry only a photocopy
Memorize your license number instead
questions.explanation: You are required to have your valid permit or driver license with you whenever you operate a vehicle.

questions.prompt: With a Tennessee Intermediate Restricted License, you may not drive between:
choices: 11 p.m. and 6 a.m.
10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
Midnight and 6 a.m.
9 p.m. and 5 a.m.
questions.explanation: Intermediate license holders are restricted from driving between 11 p.m. and 6 a.m.

questions.prompt: Tennessee's implied consent law means that by driving you agree to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs if lawfully arrested
Random vehicle searches
Pay all tickets immediately
Waive your right to insurance
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, driving means you have agreed to BAC or drug testing after a lawful DUI arrest.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test after a lawful DUI arrest in Tennessee can result in:
choices: License suspension
No consequences
A warning only
A reduced speeding fine
questions.explanation: Refusing the test under the implied consent law leads to a license suspension separate from any DUI penalty.

questions.prompt: Tennessee has a primary seat belt law, which means an officer can:
choices: Stop and ticket you solely for not wearing a seat belt
Only ticket you if you commit another violation
Never ticket for seat belts
Ticket only the driver, not passengers
questions.explanation: A primary seat belt law allows officers to stop a vehicle just for a seat belt violation.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, children ages 4 through 8 who are under 4 feet 9 inches tall must ride in:
choices: A belt-positioning booster seat
A rear-facing seat only
The front seat with a lap belt
No restraint
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires children ages 4 to 8 under 4 feet 9 inches tall to use an approved booster seat.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many points on your Tennessee driving record can lead to:
choices: License suspension
A free defensive driving course only
Higher speed limits
Automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: Too many points within a set period can trigger a suspension and driver-improvement requirements.

questions.prompt: A first DUI conviction in Tennessee can result in:
choices: Jail time, fines, and loss of driving privileges
Only a warning
A small parking fine
No license impact
questions.explanation: Even a first DUI carries serious penalties including possible jail, fines, and license loss.

questions.prompt: Reckless driving in Tennessee is:
choices: A serious offense that can bring fines and points
Always just a warning
Legal on interstates
Never enforced
questions.explanation: Reckless driving is treated as a serious violation that can lead to fines, points, and other penalties.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you should:
choices: Stop, help the injured, and exchange information
Leave the scene immediately
Move on if damage seems minor
Drive away and report it next week
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is a serious offense; you must stop, assist anyone hurt, and exchange information.

questions.prompt: In Tennessee, who is responsible for making sure young children are properly restrained?
choices: The driver
The youngest passenger
Only the parents at home
No one
questions.explanation: The driver is legally responsible for ensuring that children in the vehicle are correctly restrained.

questions.prompt: If a police officer signals you to pull over, you should:
choices: Signal, slow down, and stop safely at the right side of the road
Speed up to find a better spot
Ignore the officer if you did nothing wrong
Stop suddenly in the travel lane
questions.explanation: You must pull over safely and promptly when an officer signals you to stop.
